LHX.sentence: L3Harris barely budged today — the real thing to watch is that their profit margins still run well below the other big defense contractors, and that's the gap they need to close to prove the story.

ASML.sentence: ASML has rallied hard lately and is sitting well above its recent average price, which is just the market getting excited about the only company that makes these advanced chipmaking machines.

LIN.sentence: Linde is quiet — its industrial gas business runs on steady long-term contracts, though a close competitor has edged ahead of it over the past month.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh data on Microsoft today, but the AI cloud story keeps grinding along without drama.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher slipped a little, and it looks like its lab-supply business is bottoming out after customers spent a while working down stockpiles — the signal to watch is when demand starts picking back up.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge eased back slightly; this pipeline toll-road pays a nice dividend, but the stock looks fully priced right now rather than cheap.

NOW.sentence: No data on ServiceNow today, but big companies and government agencies keep signing on for its workflow-automation and AI tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore ticked up a bit and looks poised to catch up to the broader copper rally, since copper prices have been surging while this miner has lagged behind.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin has fallen hard and is sitting at a fresh low for the year, well below its longer-term average — a rough patch, though some see beaten-down prices like this as an opportunity.

KOF.sentence: This Latin American Coca-Cola bottler was essentially flat, and it looks reasonably cheap for a steady business that pays a decent dividend.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras dipped slightly and is still paying a hefty dividend, but its unusually cheap-looking price is actually a warning that oil earnings may be near a peak and could roll over.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish container-shipping giant that moves goods around the world by sea, had a good day and is trading below the value of its own ships — cheap for a company with that much physical fleet.
